trans-unit.target: We use essential cookies to run this app and optional monitoring cookies to improve reliability.
Required for authentication, security and core functionality. This category is always active.
Choose which optional cookie categories may be used.
Currently this category uses Sentry for application and error monitoring.
Helps us monitor application stability and error rates.
Review participating hospitals and jump into their latest allocation details.
Browse the full hospital directory and discover participating facilities.
Browse allocations, indications, transports, and other source data in one place.
Start with the statistics overview before diving into detailed analyses.
Upload a new allocation import and keep your dataset up to date.
Follow updates, guidance, and background stories from the project team.
New Import has been created successfully!
You are now signed in. Please verify your email address to unlock all account features.
Registration successful. Please verify your email address.
Your email address has been verified.
There was a problem validating your reset request.
Please confirm your cookie settings before signing in.

Email address updated. Please verify your new email.
Sorry there are no hospitals assigned to your account.
Try adjusting your search or filter to find what you're looking for.
Username is fixed and cannot be changed.
Overview of all available data records
Emergency service deployments recorded in IVENA with their details.
Indicates who initiated the hospital selection, e.g. patient, physician, or emergency service.
Specialized clinical units within a medical field, e.g. Cardiology or General Surgery.
Regions covered by emergency dispatch centers
Facilities delivering medical care and patient services.
Preliminary suspected diagnosis summarizing the primary medical problem or injury at the scene.
Potential infectious diseases that may require isolation at the destination hospital.
Mass casualty incidents recorded in IVENA with their associated operational and medical details.
Origins or contexts of an emergency case, such as public space, doctor’s office, or home environment.
Secondary transport types for inter-hospital or follow-up transfers.
Overarching medical fields such as Internal Medicine or Surgery.

Although emergency medical services are among the largest referrers to emergency departments, there are still only few large-scale systematic analyses of EMS patient characteristics. Since 2017, the web-based IVENA system has been used across Germany to digitally register EMS patients at acute care hospitals.

For every allocation, an anonymized dataset is created and stored, including variables such as age, sex, coded suspected diagnosis, urgency, assignment pathway, physician accompaniment, CPR, ventilation, infection status, pregnancy, and requested resources like resuscitation room or cath lab.

In early 2021, members of the DGINA working group in Hesse initiated this collaborative platform to merge IVENA allocation data from many hospitals and enable shared statistics and dedicated analyses for specific emergency care research questions.
Generate distributions and trend views to support evidence-based emergency care decisions.
Inspect records with filters for urgency, transport context, assignment patterns, and clinical characteristics.
Participating sites can manage institutions, maintain metadata, and work with their own allocation exports.
The initiative is run by an independent clinical research network with a clear public-health focus.
Analyze age, sex, coded suspected diagnosis, priority, assignment pathway, and other operational characteristics across large cohorts.
Each hospital can be represented with structured profile data, participation status, and ownership assignments for local administration.
Hospitals can continuously import new IVENA exports and use structured exports for downstream scientific workflows.
Create focused analyses for specific research questions and compare institutional patterns with anonymized clusters.
Built for hospitals, research teams, and quality improvement initiatives.
Placeholder image: final visual screenshot will be added next.
Participating hospitals can combine IVENA allocation data, run shared analyses, and compare anonymized benchmarks in one place.
We collaboratively build emergency care research statistics in Germany based on IVENA allocation data.
The platform is free of charge.
Participation is open to interested institutions and users. Register to learn more and access the collaborative workspace.
A quick snapshot of the current collaborative data network.
No rows with known age for this selection; box plot is not shown.
Age distribution by category (min, quartiles, median, max)
Detailed cohort distribution (mean age & box plot)
Statistics → Distribution → Age cohorts: table, filters, and box plot by category.
The projection has no rows yet. Import data and rebuild the allocation stats projection to see charts here.
Select an import to view allocation distributions from the projection.
Aggregated across the full allocation stats projection: %count% rows total.

This form allows you to link a raw indication with a corresponding normalized indication from the standardized list. By selecting the appropriate normalized indication, you help ensure consistent data classification and make further analysis and reporting more accurate.
If your account exists and your email is verified, a password reset link was sent.
Manage your account profile, email, and password.
The selected state does not have enough hospitals in the statistics data. Showing public statistics instead.
The selected dispatch area does not have enough hospitals in the statistics data. Showing public statistics instead.
The selected cohort has fewer than 2 participating hospitals. Showing public statistics instead.
The “virtual remainder” assumes cases without any listed flag; overlapping flags can make segment sums exceed the total, so stacked percentages may exceed 100%.
Most frequent diagnosis labels in the selected scope and period.
Detailed KPIs, distributions and top lists for your hospitals will appear here in a future release.
{user, number, integer} Allocations (of {total, number, integer}, {percent, number, :: .#}%)
{percent, number, :: .#}% of {total, number, integer} allocations in this period
Showing all hospitals — no clinics are assigned to your account.
Table widgets will be available here later.

This blog shares product updates, release notes and project insights from the Collaborative IVENA Statistics team.
Only published posts with a timestamp in the past become publicly visible.
{count, plural, =0 {no comments} one {# comment} other {# comments}}
